summary: Re-tested the page-level MemberBadge strip wrappers after the main agent wrapped each MemberBadge usage in a <div data-testid=...>. 4 of the 5 page strips now resolve correctly. /my-money still does NOT expose 'my-money-member-strip' for an admin who lands on /my-money without ?kid=<child_id>, because the page short-circuits into the 'Admin index' branch (showAdminIndex) which renders the kids-list view and never includes the MemberBadge strip — the strip is only inside the 'Personal ledger' return block. Avatar dialog testids on /family-members all present (upload, file-input, and remove appears after a file is selected). Welcome toast fires once for Alice on first /wall-board visit ('Welcome back, Alice') and is gone on revisit (sessionStorage flag works); fresh toast 'Welcome back, Theresa' fires after switching to Theresa. Admin chip behaves correctly: present in wall-member-strip / budget-member-strip / family-members-member-strip for admin Alice and absent (count=0) for non-admin Theresa on /wall-board. tp-member-strip intentionally hides the admin chip (compact=true, by design — already noted in iteration_7).

ui_bugs.issue:
When an admin opens /my-money without ?kid=, MyMoney.jsx returns the admin-index JSX (showAdminIndex=true, lines ~295–340) which does NOT render
. The wrapper currently only lives inside the 'Personal ledger' return at line 351. Result: 'my-money-member-strip' is missing for admins on the index view. Fix: add the same wrapper inside the admin-index branch (right under
in the max-w-md container), or move the strip into the shared
component.

action_items:
Add
inside the admin-index branch of MyMoney.jsx (currently returns at line ~296). Alternatively render it once at the top of the page above the showAdminIndex conditional. After that, all 5 page strips will resolve in the DOM for an admin without a ?kid param.
critical_code_review_comments: MyMoney has two return branches (admin index vs personal ledger). The MemberBadge strip is duplicated logic — extracting it into a shared header would prevent future regressions like this one.
tp-member-strip never contains member-badge-admin-chip because MemberBadge is in compact mode on TimePlan; that is intentional but worth documenting so test cases don't assert the chip there.

success_rate.frontend: 92% — 11/12 expectations met. Pass: wall-member-strip, tp-member-strip, budget-member-strip, family-members-member-strip all contain member-badge-name='Alice'; admin chip present on wall/budget/family-members for Alice and absent (count=0) on /wall-board for non-admin Theresa; welcome toast fires once for Alice on first /wall-board visit and is gone on revisit; switching to Theresa fires a fresh 'Welcome back, Theresa' toast; avatar-upload-btn + avatar-file-input visible inside Add-member dialog; avatar-remove-btn appears after a file is uploaded. Fail: my-money-member-strip missing on /my-money for admin without ?kid (admin-index branch doesn't render the wrapper).

test_credentials: Fresh per run via POST /api/auth/register {family_name, email, password, confirm_password}. NOTE: response key is 'access_token' (not 'token' or 'account_token'). Adding a SECOND member with the account_token returns 403 — must select the admin member first and use the resulting member access_token to add additional members. Bootstrap used: Alice (admin, PIN 1234, avatar=tiny PNG data URL) + Theresa (adult non-admin, PIN 2222).
seed_data_creation: Single fresh family TEST_AV2_<ts> per run with Alice (admin+avatar) and Theresa (non-admin).
context_for_next_testing_agent: POST /api/auth/register returns {access_token, account, family}. POST /api/auth/member/select returns {access_token, member} — use member token in Authorization header for non-trivial mutations (adding additional members beyond the first one returns 403 with the bare account token). Drive auth in the SPA by writing localStorage: mfml_account_token, mfml_member_token, mfml_account, mfml_family, mfml_member, mfml_auth_ok='true', mfml_lang. Clear sessionStorage before /wall-board to see the 'Welcome back, <name>' toast (sessionStorage key mfml_welcomed_<member_id>=1). For the admin path on /my-money to render the personal-ledger branch (and thus the my-money-member-strip wrapper as currently coded), use /my-money?kid=<child_id> with a real child member in the family. Or, after the fix, the strip will appear in the admin-index branch too.
rca of the issue:
MyMoney.jsx has two render branches. Branch A (lines ~295–340) is 'showAdminIndex' (admin AND no ?kid param) and renders a kids-list. Branch B (lines ~343+) is the personal ledger and contains
at line 351. The other four pages (WallBoard, TimePlan, HomeBudget, FamilyMembers) have only one render path so the wrapper is always present. Fix: render the strip wrapper above the showAdminIndex conditional, or duplicate it inside the admin-index
block right after
.
